# cloudcix_compute_ssh_key (Resource)

Management of SSH Key records.

This module provides API endpoints for managing SSH Key pairs used when provisioning
compute instances. SSH Keys are stored in the Membership service and proxied through here.

Available operations:
- List SSH Keys belonging to the requesting User's Address
- Create a new SSH Key (optionally auto-generate an Ed25519 key pair)
- Read a single SSH Key record
- Delete an SSH Key record

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cloudcix_compute_ssh_key" "example_compute_ssh_key" {
  name = "my-laptop-key"
  public_key = "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAINjHZddfHd1Y2rXPzX7XuJz0tVJ7m1E+71FbhJ8H29BJ"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) A unique, user-friendly name for the SSH Key. Maximum 50 characters.

### Optional

- `public_key` (String) Optional. The SSH public key to store. Must begin with a recognised key-type prefix (e.g. ssh-ed25519, ssh-rsa). If omitted, an Ed25519 key pair is generated automatically and the private key is returned once in the create response.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) Timestamp, in ISO format, of when the SSH Key record was created.
- `id` (Number) The ID of the SSH Key record.
- `private_key` (String) The PEM-encoded Ed25519 private key. Only present in the create (POST) response
when no public_key was supplied and the key pair was auto-generated. Not stored
by the API — save it immediately.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import cloudcix_compute_ssh_key.example '<id>'
```
